message:
Update dependencies and sync beads issue tracker

Bump workspace and wa crate dependency versions. Sync beads tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","title":"Tests/E2E: pane reservations (TTL, conflicts, audit)","description":"# Task: Tests/E2E — pane reservations

## Goal
Prove reservation behavior is correct, enforced, and visible with strong artifacts.

## Unit/integration tests
- Reserve → conflict → release lifecycle.
- TTL expiry converts active reservation to expired.
- Policy enforcement returns stable error codes and reasons.

## E2E scenario
- Reserve a pane via `wa robot reserve`.
- Attempt to send from a different owner (expect deny/require-approval).
- Release reservation and send again (expect allow).

## Artifacts
- Structured logs
- Audit trail extract (redacted)
- JSON outputs for robot commands

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conflicts are deterministic and explainable.
- Audit records include reservation metadata (owner, reason, expires_at).

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure the E2E runner can intentionally fail the case when TTL expiry is not honored.
  - Assert artifacts include both the deny decision and the later allow decision.

Storage tests (28 passing in storage::reservation_tests):
- Struct: is_active (valid/expired/released), serde round-trip
- Config: defaults, TTL clamping
- Create: basic, no-reason, conflict, post-release, different panes, TTL
- Release: sets released, idempotent
- Get active: returns Some/None correctly
- List: empty, multiple panes, excludes released
- Expire: stale/valid discrimination
- Round-trip: reserve→release→reserve, expired→new

Policy tests (4 passing):
- authorize_denies_reserved_pane
- authorize_allows_owning_workflow_on_reserved_pane
- risk_matrix_reserved_pane
- pane_capabilities_reservation_prevents_input

Robot commands compile and pass (22 robot tests).
E2E scenario deferred to wa-4vx.10 registry (requires running WezTerm).","created_at":"2026-01-29T08:15:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.2","title":"[EPIC] Phase 3: Full agent support — complete packs + account rotation","description":"# Phase 3 — Full Agent Support

## Objective
Expand from “works for the main path” to “works broadly”:
- Complete patterns and workflows for **Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini**.
- Harden account rotation logic and integrate usage tracking as a first-class system.

## Why this phase exists
Agent CLIs change output formats over time; partial coverage creates fragility. This phase invests in:
- more complete detection packs
- richer structured extraction
- regression fixtures (“golden corpus”)
- workflow variants per agent

## Deliverables
- Rule packs:
  - `core.codex`: full coverage for usage warnings/reached/session summaries/auth prompts.
  - `core.claude_code`: compaction + banner + usage signals (as discovered in field).
  - `core.gemini`: usage reached + session summary + model changes + resume mechanics.

- Workflows:
  - `handle_usage_limits` implemented for all three agents (behavior may differ).
  - `handle_session_end` (capture + store structured session summaries).
  - `handle_auth_required` (centralize login flows).

- Accounts:
  - `caut` becomes the source of truth for usage; wa stores a historical mirror.
  - Selection policy (percent remaining + LRU tie-break).

## Acceptance criteria
- Primary detections for all three agents are covered by unit tests and golden corpus fixtures.
- At least one real-world end-to-end run exists for each agent’s usage-limit handling path (or documented safe fallback if truly impossible to automate).



## Success Criteria
- Rule packs and workflows expand to support all target agents (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini) with consistent behavior.
- Account rotation and agent-specific workflows are covered by regression tests and golden corpora.
- User-facing surfaces (robot/MCP/human CLI) remain stable as capabilities expand.


## Testing
- Golden corpus coverage:
  - Every new rule added in this phase must land with fixtures (positive + near-miss negative).
  - Corpus tests must fail loudly on drift and point to the exact rule + capture that changed.

- Cross-agent workflow regression tests:
  - Use shared workflow engine tests plus per-agent fixtures to validate semantics remain consistent (idempotency, resume, safe failure).

- E2E per-agent reality checks:
  - For each agent (Codex/Claude/Gemini), capture at least one real transcript and ensure it’s represented in fixtures.
  - If true automation is not possible (MFA), require an E2E “fails safe” scenario that still produces artifacts and instructions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.1","title":"[EPIC] Expand rule packs + golden corpus (Codex/Claude/Gemini)","description":"# Expand rule packs + golden corpus

## Goal
Move from "minimum viable patterns" to broad, resilient coverage.

## Why
Agents change output format over time. This epic institutionalizes:
- pack iteration discipline
- fixtures as source of truth
- low false-positive matching

## Deliverables
- Additional rules for all three agents.
- Corpus expansion and drift-handling workflow.



## Success Criteria
- Expanded packs cover real-world patterns for each agent with stable rule ids and minimal false positives.
- Golden corpus grows over time and prevents regressions across releases.
- Pack changes are validated by unit tests and corpus runner output is actionable.


## Testing
- Corpus discipline:
  - Every new rule requires fixtures that include:
    - positive matches
    - near-miss negatives
    - extraction assertions for structured facts

- Drift workflow:
  - When a real transcript drifts, the process is:
    1) add failing fixture
    2) adjust rule pack
    3) verify corpus runner + unit tests
    4) optionally add a second near-miss negative if the rule was loosened

- Tooling tests:
  - Pack linter/tests must validate rule IDs are stable, unique, and follow naming conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.2.1.1","title":"Expand core.codex rule pack + corpus (warnings, summaries, auth prompts, drift variants)","description":"# Task: Expand core.codex pack

## Goal
Increase coverage and robustness for Codex CLI outputs.

## Additions (examples)
- More usage warning formats / punctuation changes.
- Session end summary variants:
  - missing reasoning tokens
  - different ordering
  - commas/spaces changes
- Resume hint variants (`codex resume` vs alias forms).
- Device-auth variants (URL changes, prompt formatting).

## Deliverables
- New/updated rules in `core.codex`.
- Corpus fixtures for each variant.
- Negative fixtures to prevent false positives.

## Testing
- Golden corpus regression (see `wa-4vx.10.1`):
  - every new/changed rule must have at least one positive fixture
  - add negative fixtures for common false-positive traps
- Pack linter/drift workflow (see `wa-nu4.2.1.4`) enforces fixture coverage and stable IDs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Corpus suite passes and covers new variants.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.1.3","title":"Expand core.gemini rule pack + corpus (usage, session summaries, resume semantics, model changes)","description":"# Task: Expand core.gemini pack

## Goal
Capture Gemini CLI outputs robustly.

## Deliverables
- New rules for:
  - usage warnings/reached
  - session summary parsing
  - model changes
  - resume hints if present
- Fixtures to lock behavior.




## Acceptance Criteria
- New `core.gemini` rules are added for all listed deliverables (usage warnings/reached, session summary parsing, model changes, resume hints when present) with stable rule ids.
- Golden corpus fixtures are added/updated so:
  - the corpus runner fails loudly on drift
  - fixture diffs are actionable (rule id + expected vs actual)
- Known false positives in non-Gemini panes are covered by tests (agent gating/dedup semantics).


## Testing
- Fixture requirements:
  - For each new rule: positive fixture + near-miss negative fixture.
  - Include at least one “non-Gemini pane” fixture to validate agent gating.

- Drift workflow:
  - When Gemini output changes, the first step is adding a failing fixture; only then adjust patterns.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.1.4","title":"Pack tooling: `wa robot rules list/test`, pack linter, drift workflow (fixture-first)","description":"# Task: Pack tooling + drift workflow

## Goal
Make rule evolution disciplined and fast.

## Deliverables
- `wa robot rules list` (emit rule ids + metadata)
- `wa robot rules test "<text>"` (emit match trace)
- Pack linter:
  - ensure stable ids
  - ensure each rule has at least one fixture or unit test
- Documented drift workflow:
  - capture failing output
  - add fixture
  - update rule
  - ship pack bump

## Testing
- Robot schema validation:
  - `wa robot rules list` output is validated against schemas
  - `wa robot rules test` output is validated against schemas

- Linter tests:
  - fixture-based tests for:
    - stable id enforcement
    - “each rule has fixture/unit test” enforcement
    - detection of invalid/slow regex patterns

- E2E coverage:
  - covered by `wa-4vx.10.24` (rules list/test + pack linter drift workflow)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot rules list` returns a stable JSON output listing rule ids + metadata (no secrets) and is validated in tests.
- `wa robot rules test "<text>"` returns a stable JSON match trace suitable for debugging and is validated in tests.
- Pack linter exists and enforces:
  - stable ids
  - each rule has at least one fixture or unit test
  - no invalid/slow regex patterns without justification
- Drift workflow is documented and fixture-first, and contributors can follow it without consulting external docs.
","notes":"2026-01-27: COMPLETE - Implemented all deliverables:
- wa robot rules list ✓
- wa robot rules test ✓  
- wa robot rules show ✓
- wa robot rules lint ✓ (ID validation, regex checks, fixture coverage)
- Drift workflow documented in AGENTS.md ✓","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:09:03.110842494Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T02:18:40.003835961Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T02:18:40.003707382Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-patterns","area-tests","phase-3","work-done"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.1.5","title":"Agent quirks + drift catalog (field-tested)","description":"# Task: Agent quirks + drift catalog (field-tested)

## Goal
Capture **undocumented behaviors** and output drift for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini, then bake them into rule packs and regression fixtures.

This resolves PLAN.md Open Question #7 and prevents silent detection regressions.

## Scope
- Collect real-world snippets/banners/prompts that differ from current fixtures.
- Document quirks that affect detection or workflow gating:
  - unexpected banner variations
  - prompt markers missing or delayed
  - usage-limit text variants
  - alt-screen transitions

## Deliverables
1. **Quirks catalog** (short doc or section in pack notes):
   - agent
   - symptom / output pattern
   - detection impact
   - mitigation (rule update / guard change)

2. **Fixture updates**:
   - add sanitized transcripts to `tests/corpus/*`
   - include expected JSON detections

3. **Rule adjustments**:
   - update pack regexes with minimal, stable anchors
   - add negative fixtures for false positives

4. **Drift log**:
   - record date + source of drift
   - link to tests added

## Testing
- Golden corpus regression must include new fixtures.
- Add at least one negative fixture per new rule tweak.
- Ensure pack linter (`wa-nu4.2.1.4`) passes after updates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Quirks catalog covers all three agents.
- Each documented quirk is tied to at least one new fixture.
- Rule updates are minimal and do not introduce new false positives.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.4.4","title":"Cass integration tests: fixtures, missing-tool behavior, ambiguity cases","description":"# Task: Cass integration tests

## Goal
Lock down cass integration behavior so it stays reliable as external tools evolve.

## Test categories
1. **Parsing fixtures**
   - `cass query` output → `CassSession` model
   - ensure unknown fields do not break parsing

2. **Missing tool**
   - if `cass` is not installed or not executable:
     - error is classified as `MissingTool`
     - workflow surfaces actionable next steps

3. **Ambiguous correlation**
   - multiple sessions in same path within time window
   - ensure deterministic tie-break

4. **Token accounting**
   - sum token_count fields correctly
   - tolerate missing token_count entries

## Deliverables
- A small fixture set committed in-tree.
- Unit tests for wrapper + correlation + aggregation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover success + error cases.
- Lint passes (avoid flaky time-based tests; use fixed timestamps).


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure tests actually execute the code paths that spawn `cass` by using a stub binary and asserting it was invoked.
  - Assert stable error codes for MissingTool and AmbiguousCorrelation so higher layers can react deterministically.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3","title":"[EPIC] Phase 4: Polish & integration — MCP, UX, setup, diagnostics, tests","description":"# Phase 4 — Polish & Integration

## Objective
Make wa production-credible for daily use:
- MCP server (`fastmcp_rust`) parity with robot mode
- human-friendly CLI output (`rich_rust`)
- setup automation (SSH config parsing, remote mux bootstrap, idempotent wezterm.lua patching)
- observability & diagnostics surfaces (health JSON, metrics, diag bundles)
- external tool integrations (`cass`, `caut` deepening)

## Why this phase exists
Even correct automation fails without:
- introspection (why is it stuck?)
- diagnostics (what happened last hour?)
- fast operator workflows (setup and repair)

## Deliverables
- MCP server: tools + resources mapped to wa-core.
- `wa doctor` and `wa status --health`.
- Optional Prometheus endpoint for watchers.
- Diagnostic bundle generator (`wa diag bundle`).
- Setup commands: `wa setup` for local+remote canonical WezTerm mux infrastructure.
- Strong test suite + CI-friendly checks.

## Acceptance criteria
- A robot-mode agent can operate wa exclusively via MCP tools.
- A human can bootstrap a new remote host with `wa setup remote` and get persistent mux sessions.
- Diagnostic bundle captures enough to debug a stuck workflow without attaching a debugger.



## Success Criteria
- Human UX, MCP integration, setup automation, diagnostics, and ship-readiness are in place with high operator trust.
- E2E + CI runs are stable and produce artifacts; failures are diagnosable.
- The project is “installable and usable” by someone new via docs/quickstart.


## Testing
- Contract stability tests:
  - MCP parity vs robot outputs and schemas (see `wa-nu4.3.1.*`, `wa-nu4.3.1.5`).
  - Human CLI snapshot tests must avoid ANSI when not a TTY and be stable across terminals.

- Setup/ops E2E:
  - `wa setup` and `wa setup remote` must have idempotency E2E scripts with full artifacts (see `wa-nu4.3.3.*`).

- Diagnostics tests:
  - `wa doctor` fixtures cover healthy + broken states and produce predictable advice and artifact bundles.

- CI reliability:
  - CI must upload artifacts on failure and include enough logs to debug without rerunning.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.1","title":"[EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode)","description":"# [EPIC] MCP server (fastmcp_rust)

## Mission
Expose wa’s core capabilities via MCP (Model Context Protocol) so AI agents can use wa as a tool provider with:
- stable tool names
- stable JSON schemas
- low-token, machine-friendly responses
- strong safety guarantees (policy gates)

## Why MCP matters
Robot mode (`wa robot ...`) is great for shells, but MCP is the native integration surface for many agent runtimes.
A first-class MCP server makes wa more composable and enables “swarm” behaviors without brittle CLI parsing.

## Guiding principles
- **Parity with robot mode**: MCP tools call the same underlying functions as `wa robot`.
- **No new logic**: MCP is a transport / surface, not a second implementation.
- **Safe by default**: anything that sends input to panes must pass PolicyEngine.
- **Resources are read-only**: resources provide snapshots; actions are tools.

## Definition of done
- MCP server builds behind `--features mcp`.
- Tools + resources cover the Appendix A.3 surface from the plan.
- Tool outputs are deterministic and versioned.

## Notes for future-self
If parity drifts, treat it as a bug. Add tests that exercise both MCP and robot paths with the same fixtures.


## Success Criteria
- MCP tools/resources are implemented by delegating to robot/core APIs (no duplicated business logic).
- MCP responses are stable, schema-validated where applicable, and integrate with audit/policy.
- Tests cover MCP request/response parsing and tool behavior on allow/deny paths.


## Testing
- Parity tests:
  - For each MCP tool, run the equivalent robot/core call with the same fixture inputs and assert identical semantics (success shape, error codes, redaction).

- Schema/contract tests:
  - Validate MCP request/response bodies against the declared schemas.
  - Ensure stable error codes and version fields.

- Safety tests:
  - Explicit allow/deny scenarios prove PolicyEngine gates MCP actions exactly as it gates robot/human paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.1.1","title":"MCP API spec: tools/resources, schemas, error codes, versioning","description":"# Task: MCP API spec (tools/resources, schemas, errors)

## Goal
Write down a concrete MCP surface that is:
- stable (versioned)
- minimal (token-efficient)
- complete (covers required operations)
- aligned with `wa robot ...` semantics

## Deliverables
### Tool list (initial)
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- **`wa.reservations`** (list active reservations)
- **`wa.reserve`** (create reservation)
- **`wa.release`** (release reservation)

### Resource list (initial)
- `wa://panes`
- `wa://events`
- `wa://accounts`
- `wa://workflows`
- `wa://rules`
- **`wa://reservations`** (read-only view of active reservations)

### Schema requirements
- Every tool returns a stable JSON object with:
  - `ok: bool`
  - `data | error`
  - `elapsed_ms`
  - `version`
  - `now`
- Error codes are stable strings (prefix `WA-MCP-...`).

### Safety requirements
- Any tool that causes side effects must pass PolicyEngine.
- Resources are read-only snapshots.

## Testing
- The spec must be directly testable:
  - tool output schemas are validated (ideally by reusing robot schema definitions where applicable)
  - parity tests ensure MCP responses match robot/internal behavior
- The MCP tests bead (`wa-nu4.3.1.5`) should be able to reference this spec as the canonical contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A future contributor can implement the server from this spec without re-reading `PLAN.md`.
- The spec explicitly states:
  - tool names
  - required params
  - output schemas
  - error codes
- MCP parity tests and schema validations pass.


LABELS: area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.7.10: Robot JSON schemas: versioned envelope + per-command outputs, validated in tests ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.1.2: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring) ● P1

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","title":"Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring)","description":"# Task: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust)

## Goal
Add an MCP server entrypoint that can be enabled/disabled via feature flag and shares core logic with robot mode.

## Requirements
- Behind `--features mcp` (no extra deps for default build).
- Clean lifecycle:
  - init
  - register tools/resources
  - serve
  - shutdown
- Deterministic error handling + logging.

## Design notes
- Do not implement business logic in handlers; delegate to existing "robot" functions.
- Prefer a thin translation layer:
  - MCP params → internal args types
  - internal result → MCP result

## Deliverables
- `wa mcp serve` (or `wa serve-mcp`) command.
- Minimal server with one read-only tool (`wa.state`) wired end-to-end.

## Testing
- Build/feature tests:
  - `cargo check --features mcp` passes
- Integration tests (see `wa-nu4.3.1.5`):
  - server starts and responds to a basic tool call using fixtures/stubs
  - error mapping is stable (no panics)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo run --features mcp -- mcp serve` starts server without panicking.
- `wa.state` tool works end-to-end against a fixture/stub or a real WezTerm instance.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:21:03.400524995Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:01:42.089337530Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-mcp","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.1.3","title":"Implement MCP tools (full set) by delegating to robot/core APIs","description":"# Task: Implement MCP tools (full set)

## Goal
Implement the full MCP tool set defined in `wa-nu4.3.1.1` by delegating to internal/robot APIs.

## Principle
**One source of truth**: MCP handlers must call the same internal functions used by CLI/robot mode.
No duplicated parsing/validation logic.

## Tool implementation checklist
For each tool:
- Validate inputs (types, required fields)
- Call internal function
- Map result into MCP response schema
- Ensure errors are stable (`WA-MCP-...`)

Tools:
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- **`wa.reservations`**
- **`wa.reserve`**
- **`wa.release`**

## Safety
- `wa.send`, `wa.workflow_run`, and `wa.reserve` must enforce PolicyEngine.
- Consider default-deny for any new tool that could mutate state.

## Testing
- Covered by MCP tests (`wa-nu4.3.1.5`):
  - schema stability
  - parity vs robot outputs
  - policy-gated tools denied when appropriate

## Deliverables
- Tool registry wiring for all tools.
- Consistent error codes and `ok/data/error` envelope.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each MCP tool can be invoked successfully with a trivial request.
- Tools that would act on panes are rejected when policy disallows.


LABELS: area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.7: [EPIC] Robot mode CLI (stable JSON envelope + core commands) ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.1.5: Workflow runner: consume Detection events, select workflow, execute with policy gates ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.5: [EPIC] Accounts + usage integration (caut as source of truth) ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.1.9: [Robot] `wa robot workflow` (run workflow by name, stable result schema) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.2.1.4: Pack tooling: `wa robot rules list/test`, pack linter, drift workflow (fixture-first) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1.2: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.1.5: MCP tests: schema stability + parity checks vs robot outputs ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.1.6: MCP audit integration: record each tool call decision/outcome (redacted) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","title":"Implement MCP resources (wa://panes, events, accounts, workflows, rules)","description":"# Task: Implement MCP resources

## Goal
Expose read-only resources that mirror what a human would see in `wa status/events/accounts/...` but in a machine-friendly way.

## Resources
- `wa://panes` — current registry + inferred state
- `wa://events` — recent events (optionally unhandled)
- `wa://accounts` — known accounts + usage snapshot
- `wa://workflows` — available workflows + descriptions
- `wa://rules` — enabled packs + pattern list
- **`wa://reservations` — active pane reservations (owner/TTL/reason)**

## Requirements
- Resources must be **read-only**.
- Resources should be cheap to compute; prefer cached snapshots from the watcher.
- JSON output must be stable and versioned.

## Deliverables
- Resource handlers registered in MCP server.
- Pagination options where needed (events).

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.3.1.5`:
  - schema stability (versioned)
  - parity checks vs robot/human query results where applicable
  - redaction/no-secrets guarantees

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agent can browse resources without invoking tools.
- Output remains stable across versions (schema changes require version bump).


LABELS: area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.5: Persist events, agent_sessions, workflow_executions, workflow_step_log ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.4.1: Pane registry + discovery loop (wezterm list, fingerprinting, lifecycle) ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.1.1: Workflow engine types: Workflow trait, StepResult, WaitCondition, WorkflowContext ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.5: [EPIC] Accounts + usage integration (caut as source of truth) ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.2.1.4: Pack tooling: `wa robot rules list/test`, pack linter, drift workflow (fixture-first) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1.2: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.1.5: MCP tests: schema stability + parity checks vs robot outputs ● P2
